content.problemStatement:
Debug the code in the IDE to solve the problem.

The code is supposed to do the following:


Take a positive integer as input.
If it's between 1 - 7, prints the corresponding day of the week.
Else print, **Invalid input**. Check the sample test case.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the corresponding day of the week (Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday) if 1 ≤ N ≤ 7; otherwise print 'Invalid input'. Each answer is printed on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 2 maps to Tuesday and 4 maps to Thursday.
8 and 0 are out of range, 7 maps to Sunday.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by using a simple array of strings representing the days of the week. For each test case, we read an integer N and print the corresponding day of the week if N is between 1 and 7 (inclusive). Otherwise, we print 'Invalid input'.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space used does not grow with the size of the input.
